parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  startUrls: [
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/events'},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/news'},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/press-releases'},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/newsroom'},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/blog'},
    {url: 'https://' + $('Loop competitors').item.json.domain + '/resources/events'}
  ],
  crawlerType: 'playwright:adaptive',
  maxCrawlDepth: 1,
  maxCrawlPages: 22,
  maxResults: 22,
  proxyConfiguration: {useApifyProxy: true},
  removeCookieWarnings: true,
  saveMarkdown: false
}) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-fable-5', max_tokens: 8000, system: "You are a competitive-intelligence verification analyst for Simplify Healthcare's Provider1 product (provider lifecycle management for US payers: provider data management, credentialing, contracting, rosters, network adequacy, claims configuration).

You receive a research corpus for ONE competitor: Google search results (title/url/snippet), pages crawled from the competitor's own website (url + text), and recent LinkedIn company posts (text + url). The corpus may be partial.

TASK: identify industry EVENTS (conferences, summits, trade shows, forums, roundtables, webinars EXCLUDED unless flagship-hosted in-person) this competitor attended, sponsored, exhibited at, spoke at, or hosted in 2025, 2026 or 2027.

NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES:
1. Only use URLs that appear in the corpus. Never invent, guess or 'repair' a URL.
2. A CONFIRMED row requires that the text tied to that URL explicitly names (a) this competitor (any alias), (b) the event, and (c) the year. All three or it is NOT confirmed.
3. Never extrapolate: attendance in one year is NOT evidence for another year. Each year stands alone.
4. Signals that fail rules 1-3 but look plausible go to "unverified" with the hint and the reason they failed.
5. Confidence: High = event organizer page, competitor's own event/press page, or newswire explicitly stating role+event+year. Med = trade media, LinkedIn post, search snippet only.
6. status: 2025 = "Attended", 2026 = "Current", 2027 = "Upcoming" (2027 only if explicitly confirmed by a source).
7. role: choose the strongest evidenced role. Hierarchy: Host > Exhibitor > Sponsor > Speaker > Attendee > Mentioned. Include booth numbers or sponsorship tier when stated.
8. De-duplicate: one row per event+year, keep the highest-confidence source.
9. evidence_quote: a short verbatim fragment (<=25 words) from the corpus that proves the row.

OUTPUT: strict JSON only, no markdown fences, no commentary:
{"confirmed":[{"event":"","year":"2025|2026|2027","status":"","role":"","evidence_quote":"","source_url":"","confidence":"High|Med"}],"unverified":[{"event":"","year":"","hint":"","reason":""}]}
If nothing found: {"confirmed":[],"unverified":[]}", messages: [{role:'user', content: $json.corpus}] }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const now = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 16).replace('T', ' ');
return [{ json: { RunAt: now, Note: 'P1 Competitor Event Radar run complete - 20 competitors scanned. Confirmed & Unverified tabs updated. Verification: URL-retrieved sources only, no year extrapolation.' } }];
